name: Motherhood
shortDescription: Motherhood represents nurturing, creation, and the generative power of life. It embodies the qualities of care, protection, and unconditional love.
description: In the context of Tarot, Motherhood is a profound concept that extends beyond the biological act of giving birth. It signifies the nurturing and generative aspects present in all creation, encompassing the ability to foster growth, provide care, and offer unconditional love. It is closely tied to the archetype of the Great Mother, a universal symbol found in various mythologies, representing the source of all life and abundance. Motherhood in Tarot reflects the qualities of empathy, compassion, and the capacity to provide a safe and supportive environment for others to flourish. It embodies the principles of fertility, not only in the literal sense but also in the metaphorical sense of creating new ideas, projects, and opportunities. The concept is often associated with abundance, reflecting the plentiful resources and nourishment that a mother provides. It represents the cycle of life, death, and rebirth, mirroring the constant process of creation and renewal that characterizes the natural world. Motherhood is a powerful force, capable of shaping individuals and societies, and is essential for maintaining balance and harmony within the world. It is also connected to the idea of protection, safeguarding loved ones from harm and ensuring their well-being. In essence, motherhood represents the life-giving, nurturing, and protective force that sustains and fosters growth in all aspects of existence.

sources.title: The First Book of the Edda
sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
